title: Coelogyne woodiana
intro: Coelogyne woodiana is a species of orchid native to Southeast Asia, known for its unique, white flowers with a distinctive yellow lip. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, as well as its ability to stimulate the immune system and exhibit antioxidant activity. The bioactive constituents of Coelogyne woodiana have been found to include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Coelogyne woodiana is often used to make herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat a range of ailments, including respiratory infections and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Coelogyne woodiana is classified as a species of orchid in the genus Coelogyne. This genus is further grouped within the subtribe Coelogyninae, which is a part of the tribe Areinae. Coelogyne woodiana specifically belongs to the family Orchidaceae. The Orchidaceae family is one of the largest families of flowering plants, with over 30,000 known species. Coelogyne woodiana is native to the Himalayan region and is a relatively rare species.
